text.title: rust-testing-integration Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for rust-testing-integration. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use tower::ServiceExt::oneshot to drive Axum routers in integration tests. Never bind a real TCP listener on a fixed port (e.g., 127.0.0.1:8080) for HTTP integration tests. If a real listener is needed, bind to :0 and discover the assigned port with local_addr().
rule.rationale: Hardcoded ports cause CI failures when tests run in parallel — two tests bind the same address and one panics. oneshot drives the router in-process with no socket allocation.

rule.statement: Use testcontainers-rs for any integration test that requires a real database. Never point integration tests at a shared dev or staging database. Provide per-test schema isolation (CREATE SCHEMA test_<uuid> + DROP after) when sharing a container across a test suite.
rule.rationale: Integration tests that share a real Postgres container without isolation fail in CI in random order because one test's INSERT is visible to another's SELECT. Per-test schema or transaction rollback prevents state leak.

rule.statement: Run database migrations (sqlx migrate run or diesel migration run) inside the integration test setup before any assertions. Tests MUST NOT assume migrations are pre-applied in the container.
rule.rationale: A freshly-started Postgres container has no schema. Tests that skip migration setup produce confusing "relation does not exist" errors that are hard to diagnose in CI.

rule.statement: Use insta for snapshot tests of complex structured output (HTML, large JSON, AST). Do not hand-write multi-line assert_eq! strings for outputs longer than ~10 lines.
rule.rationale: Hand-written expected strings break on every whitespace or ordering change, producing noisy diffs. insta stores the expected value in a .snap file that git tracks and reviews cleanly.
